title: P6.24: Add admin risk checks - verify privileged access controls
description:
Add privileged access control verification checks to preflight system.

**Admin Risk Checks:**
- Verify privileged accounts have MFA enabled
- Check for overprivileged accounts (too many roles)
- Identify inactive admin accounts (>90 days)
- Validate emergency access accounts exist
- Check break-glass procedures documented

**Risk Indicators:**
- Global admin without MFA: CRITICAL
- Admin account inactive >90 days: WARNING
- Break-glass accounts missing: WARNING
- Too many global admins (>5): INFO

**Remediation:**
- Provide list of at-risk accounts
- Suggest remediation actions
- Track risk score trend

**Branch:** feature/phase1-p6-task-24-admin-risk
**Acceptance:** mypy ✓, ruff ✓, pytest ✓, integration ✓, review ✓, merge ✓

description:

- RiversideRequirement: Compliance requirements with due dates and status
- RiversideMaturity: Maturity assessment scores
- RiversideDevice: Device compliance and security posture
- RiversideThreat: Threat indicators and risk scores

**Key Fields:**
- Tenant identifiers, timestamps, status enums, JSON metadata, soft delete
- SQLAlchemy ORM with proper indexes and foreign keys

title: P5.18: Add MFA gap alert triggers - notify when MFA below threshold
description:
Implement MFA gap detection and alerting when enrollment drops below target thresholds.

**Alert Logic:**
- Define target MFA enrollment (95% for users, 100% for admins)
- Calculate enrollment per tenant (HTT, BCC, FN, TLL, DCE)
- Compare current vs target enrollment
- Trigger alerts when gap exceeds 5%

**Alert Details:**
- Current enrollment percentage
- Target percentage
- Number of users without MFA
- List of admin accounts missing MFA
- Tenant-specific breakdown

title: Fix Riverside API route-service method mismatch
description:
Routes in app/api/routes/riverside.py call methods that don't exist on RiversideService:
- get_riverside_summary() -> should use get_dashboard_data()
- get_mfa_status() -> should use get_mfa_stats()  
- get_maturity_scores() -> method doesn't exist, needs to be added
- get_requirements() -> should use get_requirements_by_status()
- get_gaps() -> should use get_critical_gaps()
- sync_all() -> needs to call sync_all_tenants from riverside_sync
Affects 9 API tests.

- Requirement name and description

- Evidence status

comments.author: Tyler Granlund
comments.text:
P2.3 Complete ✅

- WorkerPool with semaphore-based concurrency (4 workers for B1 SKU)
- map() and map_with_progress() methods with progress callbacks
- TaskStatus enum (PENDING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED)
- TaskResult dataclass with generic typing
- TenantProcessor with backfill_tenant() and backfill_all_tenants()
- Error isolation per tenant (one failure doesn't stop others)
- Timeout handling (3600s default)
- Retry logic with exponential backoff
- Statistics collection and summary generation
- Convenience function backfill_tenants_parallel()
- 38 comprehensive unit tests - all passing
- Merged to feature/backfill-infrastructure

title: P2.5: Create app/api/routes/riverside.py - REST API endpoints
description:
Create REST API endpoints in app/api/routes/riverside.py for Riverside compliance CRUD operations.

**Endpoints Required:**
- GET /api/v1/riverside/compliance - List compliance records
- GET /api/v1/riverside/compliance/{id} - Get single record
- POST /api/v1/riverside/compliance - Create compliance record
- PUT /api/v1/riverside/compliance/{id} - Update record
- DELETE /api/v1/riverside/compliance/{id} - Soft delete
- Similar for /mfa, /requirements, /maturity, /devices, /threats

**Features:**
- Pagination (limit/offset)
- Filtering by tenant and status
- Sorting options
- Response models from schemas
- Error handling with proper HTTP codes
